last_verdict.tests: 1958 passed, 25 skipped (.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q). FIVE waves + a LIVE test this session. Wave 5: LIVE-validated asr-tts-2 (async second pass ENGAGED, 0 underflows/exceptions/crashes/self-interrupts, barge-in cuts real talk-overs) BUT surfaced two problems: (1) the MIC IS CLIPPING 36-65% of blocks (peak=1.0, rms~0.5) -> OS mic gain too hot -> garbled STT + SenseVoice hallucinations ('I.'); OWNER must lower OS mic input level/disable mic-boost (input_gain=1.0 so it's not code). (2) owner CONFIRMED real acoustic OUTPUT artifacts (buzzy/glitchy TTS, not just garbled words) = the run-103630 white-noise still partly present; _audio_cb silently zero-fills FIFO underruns. ADDED DIAGNOSTICS (real-time-safe, reviewed SHIP): input-clipping warning in the capture heartbeat (clip=% + one-shot WARN >2%), output-underrun counter in _audio_cb (per-reply WARN + playback_underrun metric + heartbeat underruns=N). NEXT: owner lowers mic gain + re-runs -> clip% should ~0 (STT jumps) and underruns=N confirms whether the buzzy artifacts are FIFO underruns from CPU contention (async SenseVoice worker + TTS synth on the desktop CPU tier) -> if so, per-tier TTS/worker thread budgeting. Also: auto-detect picked 'desktop' not 'desktop_gpu_4090' (probe reads 16376MB < 16GB threshold -- recommend_profile off-by-rounding). --- Earlier waves 1-4: Wave 4 (low-value headless cleanup): llm-inference-9 (LlamaCppLLM type_k/type_v KV-cache quant plumbing; phone/phone_lite quantize the K cache to q8_0, V left at f16 since V-quant needs flash-attn; fail-soft on an old lib) + control-plane-2 (load-elastic admission: supervisor caps concurrency to 1 under sustained load via an ungated load_fraction reader threaded app->runtime->supervisor, separate from the live_routing-gated load_snapshot). DEFERRED with reasons: llm-inference-6 (per-call timeout blocked by the single-context lock; supervisor 25s reap backstops it), llm-inference-7 (Q3 weights config flip needs on-device quality validation). Reviewed (1 refuted, nits+1 LOW; LOW fixed -> K-only). --- Wave 3: Wave 3 (headless): control-plane-3 -- EWMA-scaled ADAPTIVE watchdog deadlines. The recorder now folds a TTS first-audio EWMA (recent_tts_ms) alongside the TTFT one; the watchdog's 'llm stuck'/'tts stuck' deadlines = clamp(4*recent_ewma, floor, ceil) with cold-start fallback to the static 10s/5s. LLM floor 6s (above a heavy-tier first-token, since the EWMA blends tiers), ceil 30s; ADAPTIVE_DEADLINES toggle pins legacy. Net: fast device surfaces a real stall sooner, slow phone stops false-flagging honest long turns (attacks the stuck:1 bundle noise). Diagnosis only -- supervisor task-reap is the real hang bound. Adversarially reviewed (0 refuted, nits+1 LOW, fixed). Scouted first: routing-cascade-6 already neutralized by LLMCapabilityRouter cache, routing-cascade-7 done, routing-cascade-4/asr-tts-3 need real models/audio. --- Wave 1+2: Wave 1: asr-tts-2 (async second-pass ASR) + asr-tts-1 (per-tier ASR policy). Wave 2 (headless, no live test): llm-inference-3 (LlamaCppLLM num_predict->max_tokens cap translation + phone/phone_lite caps) + rc-5 (SUPERSEDED metric so newest-input-wins-preempted turns don't false-flag 'llm stuck') + invariant hardening (no profile may disable speaker_gate_input / agent_brain.require_owner_verified). Both waves adversarially reviewed; wave-2 review CAUGHT A BLOCKER (a config _comment key leaking into llama.cpp's strict create_chat_completion signature) -> fixed by dropping _-prefixed option keys + a strict-signature regression test. Scouted Phase-1 first: device-adapt-1/cross-platform-8/cloud-invariant were already done.
last_verdict.current: 1988 passed, 24 skipped (.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q). 2026-06-18 (pt2): Built tools/autotest/ -- an AUTONOMOUS (no-human, no real mic) end-to-end test harness for STT/TTS/barge-in/memory, using a SMALL llm (ollama/gemma3:4b by default; echo for fast plumbing). Fills the gap between tools.live_session (needs a person) and --engine replay (only re-plays recordings). Four tiers (python -m tools.autotest memory|voice|replay|suite|all), each PASS/FAIL + JSON under logs/autotest/ (gitignored). memory: fact->distractors->recall over the REAL assistant.answer capability + in-RAM SessionMemory (no Postgres) -- proves recall is injected AND the model uses it. voice (the novel tier): stands up a PipeWire module-null-sink VIRTUAL CABLE, runs the real sherpa engine routed onto it (the runtime's OWN streams only -- moved by ALSA-PipeWire bridge node name since those streams carry no PID; system default untouched, reversible), injects TTS-synth'd 'user' utterances via paplay, reads --debug stdout live ([live] engine running / speaking: / barge-in detected / dropping self-echo final). Exercises the real-time capture thread + _audio_cb FIFO + AEC + barge-in that replay/sandbox can't. replay: wraps delay-independent tools.replay_barge (coherence) + tools.aec_probe (ERLE/delay). suite: existing headless barge/sandbox/memory pytest. CANONICAL RUN (gemma3:4b): VERDICT PASS 3/3. memory PASS ('You said your favorite color is teal'); voice PASS pipeline -- transcripts ACCURATE over the cable ('capital of France'->'Paris', sailor story, planets), S3 talk-over CUT the reply, S2 self-interrupt delay-independent coherence=0 + AEC ERLE +9-19dB; suite 56 passed. CAVEAT (documented): the cable is a DIGITAL loopback (~tens-of-ms ref delay) vs the open speaker's ~260ms acoustic delay (aec_ref_delay_ms), so the live residual-path barge SELF-FIRES once per reply as a loopback ARTIFACT (not the open-speaker bug) -- aec_probe measures the loopback at 0-80ms. So the verdict gates only on robust signals (audio flowed + round-trip + bundle) and the AUTHORITATIVE self-interrupt signal is the delay-INDEPENDENT coherence probe (read 0). --calibrate/--aec-delay-ms align AEC to the loopback for a fair live check (per-launch delay noisy->advisory). Harness = autonomous REGRESSION coverage; it does NOT replace the human-at-the-mic open-speaker run. Purely additive (new tools/autotest/ + 1 .gitignore line); no existing code touched. Handoff: docs/session_2026-06-18_autonomous_test_harness.md. --- pt3 (ENHANCED, owner request): tools.autotest voice now does REAL-VOICE injection (--utterances DIR + manifest.json of file/text/role; default synth) + WER STT scoring (tools/autotest/score.py) + THREE acoustic modes (--acoustics): cable (default, SILENT, playback->dead sink so NO echo -> clean reproducible STT/WER ~0.13-0.33, the near-field STT-accuracy path; no self-interrupt/barge since no echo), delay (SILENT, 2 sinks + ~260ms module-loopback air-gap so AEC aligns -> self-interrupt=0 + barge-in fires), speaker (REAL OVER-THE-AIR, MAKES SOUND, --make-sound; owner CONSENTED to sound+mic -> self-interrupt=0 with the REAL ~260ms acoustic delay = autonomous open-speaker result). Key learnings: the assistant TTS raises the engine's learned echo-floor which DROPS quiet far-field clips as 'echo/ambient' (near-field user is louder -> passes; injected clips avoid echo=cable or out-shout it=gain boost); the digital cable delay JITTERS per launch (0-120ms) so a fixed AEC delay can't align it -> cable went echo-free for STT, echo story lives in delay/speaker. speaker STT degraded with SYNTH voice (far-field, doubly degraded) -> owner's REAL recordings will score better; cable is the STT number that predicts the app. Files: tools/autotest/{acoustics,clips,score}.py + voice_loop/__main__ reworked. Suite green 1991 passed, 24 skipped.

next: 2026-06-18 pt3: autotest ENHANCED with real-voice injection + over-the-air (see tools/autotest/README.md + docs/session_2026-06-18_autonomous_test_harness.md). Run it any session WITHOUT the mic: `.venv/bin/python -m tools.autotest all --llm ollama --model gemma3:4b` (memory+voice+suite, ~5min) or per-tier (memory ~3s, voice ~4min, replay <1min, suite ~13s). It gives autonomous regression coverage of STT/TTS/barge-in/memory + the coherence self-interrupt defense. STILL OPEN (unchanged, needs a HUMAN at the mic -- the harness covers regression, not this final gate): the open-speaker self-interrupt P1. (1) Owner runs `./session.sh --llm echo` on the open speaker -> confirm self-interrupt is GONE live, DTLN AEC behaves, STT cleaner; it re-records a mic+FarEndRing-ref bundle. (2) THEN calibrate aec_ref_delay_ms HEADLESSLY: `python -m tools.aec_probe logs/runs/run-<new>.wav --backend dtln --device desktop --max-delay-ms 800` -> set aec_ref_delay_ms (placeholder 260) to the best-ERLE delay. (3) Residual STT garble on LOUD speech = mic clips at the ADC (hot OS gain) -- only lowering OS mic helps. OPTIONAL HARNESS UPGRADE: insert a controlled ~260ms latency into the cable (module-loopback latency_msec) so the digital path matches the configured acoustic delay -> then the LIVE self-interrupt count becomes a fair gate too (not just the coherence probe). MINOR FINDING: with the loopback misaligned, one assistant story phrase ('jack in hand') was recognized as a USER final + echoed back (self_echo_drops=0) -- a self-echo-as-input leak the 'dropping self-echo final' defense missed; re-check once AEC is delay-matched. config.local.json: aec_enabled=true aec_backend=dtln aec_ref_delay_ms=260 asr_final_backend=sense_voice asr_final_async=true input_agc=FALSE record_playback_reference=true.
